mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate levels increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows and raise corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative following high FEDFUNDS readings over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate creates a restrictive financial environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations simultaneously, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series FEDFUNDS signal by more than 50% in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of high consumer price inflation indicates supply-side overheating that prompts aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rate effects dominate output growth.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases and CPIAUCSL is above 300 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in retail sales signals immediate consumer spending weakness that reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1400 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment that spills over into broader construction and employment weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1400 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in payroll employment and retail sales creates a negative feedback loop where falling incomes reduce consumer spending, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling PAYEMS and falling RSAFS fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of co-occurring instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive quarterly contractions in real GDP signal a recessionary collapse in aggregate demand that leads to sustained equity drawdowns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth prints are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.
